message:
feat(pager,btree,vdbe,mvcc): CAS-based flat hash page cache, rightmost leaf cache, record header templates, and commit combiner one-shot allocation

Page cache (bd-eorms):
- Add FlatPageSlots: a CAS-based flat hash page cache modeled after C
  SQLite's pcache1. Uses compare-and-swap on per-slot page-number words
  so cache misses are completely lock-free (only atomic reads). Cache
  hits acquire a fine-grained per-slot mutex instead of the coarser
  per-shard mutex. Power-of-2 capacity with linear probing, tombstone
  support, and configurable max probe length (32).
- Add SLOT_EMPTY/SLOT_TOMBSTONE sentinels, MAX_PROBE_LENGTH, and
  FLAT_SLOTS_MIN_CAPACITY constants.

B-tree cursor (bd-udl9m):
- Replace append_hint_cache with rightmost_leaf_cache: a structured
  RightmostLeafCacheEntry that caches the rightmost leaf page and its
  max rowid. Sequential inserts can skip a full root-to-leaf descent
  plus leaf binary search by trying the cached page directly. Cache
  is updated after successful right-edge inserts and cleared
  conservatively on mutations that could invalidate the right edge.

VDBE codegen:
- Add precomputed record header templates (P4::RecordHeaderTemplate)
  for INSERT operations. When all columns have statically-known serial
  types, the record header is computed at codegen time and emitted as
  a P4 operand, avoiding per-row header computation at execution time.
- Add compute_record_header_size() and try_build_insert_record_header_template()
  helpers. The codegen falls back to P4::Affinity for tables with
  dynamically-typed columns.

Types:
- Add P4::RecordHeaderTemplate variant to the opcode enum.
- Refactor serialize_record_iter/serialize_record_iter_into to use a
  single unified implementation (serialize_record_iter_into_impl) that
  writes directly into a caller-supplied buffer, reducing allocations.

MVCC commit combiner:
- Add alloc_one_shot(): a one-shot commit sequence allocation method
  that claims a temporary slot, submits via flat combining, and releases.
  Avoids the need for pre-registered handles when callers don't maintain
  per-thread state.
- Add optional active_registry (Arc<Mutex<SmallVec<[u64; 16]>>>)
  for batch-registering allocated sequences atomically with allocation,
  closing the gap where finish_commit_seq could advance stable_commit_seq
  past an allocated-but-unregistered sequence.

Connection (bd-m1nte / I2):
- Adaptive retained autocommit flush thresholds: 256 pure writes vs
  16 for mixed read/write workloads, replacing the fixed 64-statement
  threshold. Tracks per-batch write and read counts separately to
  detect workload mix and choose the appropriate threshold.

WAL:
- Minor additions to WAL layer supporting the new page cache integration.

Tests:
- Add Session 75 conformance oracle tests (s75a-s75t): multi-column
  UPDATE arithmetic, INSERT RETURNING expressions, window functions
  (NTILE, CUME_DIST, PERCENT_RANK), and more.
- Add conformance oracle S55 tests.
- Add v2 superinstruction tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -4815,6 +4815,8 @@ mod tests {
                 result_row_materialization_time_ns_total: 2_500,
                 make_record_calls_total: 3,
                 make_record_blob_bytes_total: 48,
+                insert_append_count: 0,
+                insert_seek_count: 0,
                 decoded_value_types: ValueTypeMetricsSnapshot {
                     total_values: 10,
                     nulls: 1,

@@ -1,4 +1,21 @@
 //! WAL checksum primitives, integrity helpers, and native commit protocol.
+//!
+//! Fault Matrix (`bd-966ja`) for batched append and publish paths:
+//!
+//! This matrix covers the byte-oriented append helpers in [`wal::WalFile`] and
+//! the current durable-publish helper [`group_commit::write_consolidated_frames`].
+//! It intentionally stops at WAL durability. Later SHM / snapshot-plane publish
+//! remains the caller's responsibility (see `native_commit`).
+//!
+//! | Stage | Primary APIs | Fault conditions | Surface | State after fault |
+//! | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
+//! | Batch shape + serialization | `prepare_frame_bytes_with_transforms_into` | Frame-count mismatch, page-size mismatch, `frame_count * frame_size` overflow, salt/checksum helper failure while constructing transforms | `WalCorrupt`, `DatabaseFull`, or helper error | WAL file and in-memory WAL counters stay unchanged; caller-owned scratch may contain partial serialized bytes |
+//! | Append-window validation | `prepared_append_window_still_current` | File size changed, generation header changed, short header read, header parse failure | `Ok(false)` for stale window; `WalCorrupt` / parse error for malformed WAL | No append occurs; caller must rebuild from the new seed or treat the WAL as corrupt |
+//! | Checksum finalization | `finalize_prepared_frame_bytes` | Prepared buffer length mismatch, `frame_count * frame_size` overflow, salt/checksum write failure | `WalCorrupt`, `DatabaseFull`, or checksum helper error | No on-disk mutation; caller buffer may have partially rewritten checksum fields |
+//! | Durable byte append | `append_finalized_prepared_frame_bytes` | Frame-count overflow, prepared buffer length mismatch, VFS `write` failure, post-write state advance overflow guard | `DatabaseFull`, `WalCorrupt`, or VFS error | Pre-write validation faults leave WAL state unchanged; a write failure happens before `advance_state_after_write`, so in-memory counters do not advance even though the on-disk tail may need replay/validation |
+//! | One-shot prepared append | `append_prepared_frame_bytes` | Any finalization fault plus any finalized-append fault | Propagated error from the lower stage | Same guarantees as the composed lower layers: no publish on finalize failure; write-path faults may leave an untrusted tail that recovery must trim or validate |
+//! | Fused batched append | `append_frames` | Test/fault-injection busy hook, page-size mismatch in any frame, batch-size overflow, salt/checksum helper failure, finalized append failure, test-only after-append injected fault | `Busy`, `WalCorrupt`, `DatabaseFull`, helper error, or injected test fault | Assembly faults leave `frame_scratch` restored and counters unchanged; an after-append injected fault is special because bytes/state may already be advanced even though the function returns `Err` |
+//! | Durable publish | `write_consolidated_frames`, direct `WalFile::sync` callers | Frame-batch byte-size overflow, any `append_frames` fault, `sync(FULL)` failure after a successful append | `Internal`, append error, or VFS sync error | If `sync` fails, WAL bytes may already be appended and `WalFile` state may already reflect them, but durability/publish is not established, so higher layers must not advertise the batch as committed |
 
 #[cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")]
 use std::path::Path;
